$comment: Machine-readable twin of the results table on https://contextbenchmark.com/#results. Served with Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * so agents, answer engines and dashboards can read it directly. The authoritative artefacts are the fingerprints in the repository; this file is a summary of them.
$reproduction: Re-run 2026-07-17 against the 2026-07-05 baseline. bm25 and emb-minilm reproduced byte-identical artifacts 12 days on, which is what their levels are supposed to mean. spiderbrain's artifact hash did not reproduce. Isolation tests (identical corpus bytes, identical embedded clock, identical absolute path) proved three independent inputs move that artifact: the git HEAD commit time of the repository enclosing the scanned project, the absolute path of the project, and the engine version. The first two are reproducibility defects in the engine's artifact serialization, found by this re-verification and logged for a fix; the third is ordinary version change, now recorded in the fingerprint's system field. All 10 query result hashes were identical under every condition, so retrieval behaviour and every reported metric are unchanged. An earlier version of this note attributed the change to the engine update alone; that was published before the cause was isolated, and was wrong.

corpus.name: micro-app
corpus.description: Committed, deterministic 12-file TypeScript fixture (layered webshop) with 10 fixed queries.

trustLevels.CTL 1: Repeatable locally. Results not identical but rank-stable (Jaccard@k >= 0.9, Kendall tau >= 0.9).
trustLevels.CTL 2: Stable retrieval. Artifact bytes differ, but ranked query results are identical every time.
trustLevels.CTL 3: Machine-deterministic. Byte-identical artifacts across rebuilds and exact-match query results across trials.
trustLevels.CTL 4: Cross-machine deterministic. CTL 3, plus identical artifacts and query results across operating systems, verified by fingerprint exchange.

results.description: Structural code-context engine (dependency graph plus frozen-clock scoring).

crossMachine.note: Pending CI-runnable packaging. Fingerprint published for independent comparison.

results.artifactHashCaveat: Recomputable only at benchmark-repo commit 2b4f057 on the build machine: the engine currently embeds the enclosing repository's git HEAD commit time and the project's absolute path in the artifact of record. Defect logged with the engine; until fixed, spiderbrain's byte-identity claim holds within a repository state, not across commits or clones. Query hashes are unaffected and recompute anywhere.

results.description: Dependency-free lexical index. The determinism reference baseline.

crossMachine.note: Verified in CI: artifact hashes identical and 10 of 10 query results identical across all three OS pairs.

results.description: MiniLM chunk embeddings with exhaustive search. The most determinism-friendly RAG configuration possible; ANN-indexed deployments are expected to do worse.

disclosure.rule: No Context Trust Level may be claimed without a published fingerprint that anyone can independently recompute and compare. Reports missing fingerprints should not be trusted, including ours.
